content.description:
Hob and JavaScript have different runtime models. These pitfalls arise at the boundary and have caused real bugs during Hob view conversions.

## Hob Functions Are Always Async

Every Hob function returns a Promise. Never pass a Hob function as a synchronous callback to JS APIs:

```javascript
// BROKEN — Array.find gets a Promise (truthy), matches first element
const found = items.find(hobPredicate);

// BROKEN — Array.sort gets Promise comparisons, breaks ordering
items.sort(hobComparator);

// BROKEN — getItemName returns "[object Promise]"
const name = getItemName(id);
```

**Fix**: Use Hob-native equivalents (`filter` + `first`, `map`) or pre-compute values into a plain JS data structure and do synchronous lookups.

## `invoke` Arg Order

`invoke` calls a method on a JS object:

```clojure
;; Correct: obj.method(arg) 
(invoke obj "method" arg)

;; WRONG: does method[arg](), not obj.method(arg)
(invoke (dom-get obj :method) arg)
```

## `invoke` Converts Args via `hobToJs`

All arguments (but NOT the object) go through `hobToJs`, which converts non-DOM, non-primitive objects to plain `{}` by iterating `Object.entries`. This destroys browser API objects that have internal slots:

```clojure
;; BROKEN — History object loses internal slots when passed as arg
(invoke some-fn "call" null history)

;; Fix — pass browser objects as the invocation target, not as args
(invoke history "pushState" state "" url)
```

**Rule**: Never pass `History`, `Location`, `Navigator`, or other browser API objects as `invoke` arguments. Pass them as the object (first argument) instead.

## Morphdom Keeps Old Handlers

When morphdom patches the DOM after a Hob view re-render, it keeps the old element's event handlers rather than transferring new ones from the discarded `toEl` tree. This is correct because:

- morphdom keeps `fromEl` (the live DOM) and discards `toEl` (the fresh render)
- Old handlers have correct DOM references to the kept elements
- Handler logic is identical across renders; data is fetched fresh via `get-item`

**Do NOT** transfer `__hobEvents` in `onBeforeElUpdated` — new handlers capture refs to the discarded DOM tree.

## Selector Pins

Once a selector is registered via `(get-item id :select fn)` for an item, later bare `(get-item id)` calls on the same item don't widen the selector. The first selector registration is the contract.

This means: put your selector on the primary `get-item` call, not on a secondary lookup.

## `data-sort-key` for Morphdom Identity

Always add `data-sort-key` (or another stable attribute) to elements rendered in loops — wrappers, pills, list items, etc. Without this, morphdom matches elements by position, causing mismatched updates when items are reordered.

```clojure
[:div {:data-sort-key item-id} ...]
```

## Hob Functions Can't Replace Sync APIs

Some browser/JS APIs require synchronous callbacks:

- `history.pushState` / `history.replaceState` (must be called synchronously in response to user action)
- `Array.sort` comparators
- `JSON.stringify` replacers
- Event handler return values (e.g., `beforeunload`)

For these, write a JS library item that provides the synchronous interface. Example: `iframe-nav-tracker` patches `pushState` synchronously because Hob's async functions can't.

## `sexp2ast.py` Unicode Limitation

The s-expression to AST converter doesn't handle `\uXXXX` escape sequences. Use literal Unicode characters in `.hob` files instead of escape sequences.

## See Also

- [Hob Language](item://c0c0c0c0-0054-0000-0000-000000000000) — Language reference
- [Hob Recipes](item://c0c0c0c0-0056-0000-0000-000000000000) — Practical patterns
- [Hob View Conversion Tracker](item://149546fb-4cf0-4a28-a79b-7cf313286bdf) — Conversion progress and notes
- [Stale Closure Prevention](item://c0c0c0c0-0011-0000-0000-000000000000) — Related pattern for fresh data in handlers
